title: hieracium subambiguum
intro: Hieracium subambiguum, also known as the lesser hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily used to treat gastrointestinal issues and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used in herbal preparations to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating symptoms of fever and colds. Hieracium subambiguum can be consumed as a tea or infusion, or used in tinctures and salves, making it a valuable addition to herbal medicine.
